title: cuphea glareosa
intro: Cuphea glareosa, also known as the firecracker plant or Mexican heather, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant is known for its bright red, tubular flowers that resemble firecrackers, hence its common name. Cuphea glareosa has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function.
